text:
There once was a guy named Marvin who wanted to climb Mt. Hood. When he tried, he slipped and fell back down, badly injured. In despair, he thought to himself, "Why am I doing this again?" So he didn't do anything.

Months passed, and he saw his neighbor Tom climbing Mount Hood. Marvin looked up with envy. Then he had an idea—he wasn't injured anymore!

He went to Tom, stammered, "Hey Tom, could you lend me aarness? I really wanna go do that climb of yours."

Tom hesitated. "Uh, yeah. Sure?"

Marvin strapped on his harness with Tom's equipment. With renewed vigor, he climbed. He'd never have made it up the mountain without Tom's encouragement.

It turned out marvelling at the view was fun after all. So Marvin decided to climb again, climbing peaks and conquering challenges, knowing each summit brought him closer to what he truly wanted: adventure.

And Marvin learned
